description: This is Cornerstone talent data which contains employee performance data

tables.name: User_csod
tables.description: This table contains essential employee information related to talent management, including user details, managerial hierarchy, and employment status.

columns.name: fullName
columns.description: The complete name of the user, including first and last names.

business_rules: Can be null
Should not contain special characters
data_quality_checks: Check for leading or trailing spaces
Validate format to ensure it contains at least a first and last name
metadata.business_description: Represents the full name of the employee as recorded in the system, used for identification and reporting purposes.

columns.name: email
columns.description: Column representing the email address of the user.

business_rules: Can be null
Must follow standard email format
data_quality_checks: Validate email format
Check for duplicates
metadata.business_description: Email address used for communication and notifications related to employee performance and talent management.

columns.name: userId
columns.description: Identifier for users in the Cornerstone talent system

business_rules: Can be null
Should not be duplicated within the same context
data_quality_checks: Check for null values
Validate format to ensure it is a valid string
metadata.business_description: A unique identifier assigned to each user within the Cornerstone talent performance system, used to track user-related data.

columns.name: userName
columns.description: The name of the user as registered in the system.

business_rules: Can be null
Should be unique within the context of the userId
data_quality_checks: Check for duplicates against userId
Validate format to ensure it meets username standards
metadata.business_description: This column represents the username of employees within the Cornerstone talent management system, used for identification and login purposes.

columns.name: managerId
columns.description: Identifier for the manager of the user, represented as a string.

business_rules: Can be null if the user does not have a manager
Should reference a valid userId in the User_csod table if present
data_quality_checks: Check for valid userId references if managerId is not null
Ensure no invalid characters are present in the managerId
metadata.business_description: This column represents the unique identifier for the manager associated with a user in the Cornerstone talent data.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by managerId to analyze users under specific managers
Filter by userStatus to see active/inactive users under a manager

columns.name: managerName
columns.description: The name of the manager associated with the user.

business_rules: Can be null if the user does not have a manager
Should reflect the current manager's name
data_quality_checks: Ensure names are properly formatted (e.g., no numbers or special characters)
Check for consistency with managerId column
metadata.business_description: This column represents the name of the manager for each employee, which is essential for understanding reporting structures within the organization.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by user status to see active/inactive employees under a manager

columns.name: position
columns.description: Column representing the job position of the user within the organization.

business_rules: Can be null if the user is not assigned a position
Should accurately reflect the user's current job title
data_quality_checks: Ensure consistency in naming conventions
Validate against a predefined list of valid positions if available
metadata.business_description: The specific role or title held by the employee, which is relevant for performance evaluations and organizational structure.

columns.name: division
columns.description: Column representing the division within the organization to which the user belongs.

business_rules: Can be null
Should reflect the current division of the employee
data_quality_checks: Validate against known divisions
Check for consistency in naming conventions
metadata.business_description: This column indicates the specific division of the company that the employee is associated with, which can be used for organizational analysis and reporting.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by user status within division

columns.name: location
columns.description: Column representing the geographical location of the user.

business_rules: Can be null
Should contain valid location names
data_quality_checks: Check for empty values
Validate against a list of known locations
metadata.business_description: This column indicates the physical or geographical location associated with the user, which can be used for reporting and analysis of user distribution across different regions.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by user status and location

columns.name: userStatus
columns.description: Indicates the current status of the user within the system.

business_rules: Can be null
Values may include 'Active', 'Inactive', 'On Leave'
data_quality_checks: Ensure values are within the defined set of statuses
Check for null values where applicable
metadata.business_description: This column represents whether a user is active, inactive, or on leave, which is crucial for understanding user engagement and performance tracking.

columns.name: lastLogindate
columns.description: The date when the user last logged into the system.

business_rules: Can be null if the user has never logged in
Should be in a valid date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
data_quality_checks: Check for valid date format
Ensure that if a date is present, it is not in the future
metadata.business_description: This column represents the most recent date that a user accessed their account, which can be used to track user engagement and activity levels.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by user status to see active vs inactive users
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of users who logged in within a specific timeframe
MAX of last login dates to find the most recent activity

columns.name: startDate
columns.description: The date when the user started in the organization, represented as a string in date format.

business_rules: Can be null if the start date is not available
Should follow a valid date format (e.g., YYYY-MM-DD) if provided
data_quality_checks: Validate date format
Check for logical consistency with userStatus (e.g., startDate should not be after lastLogindate)
metadata.business_description: Indicates the commencement date of an employee's tenure within the organization, which is crucial for performance evaluations and tenure-related analyses.
filtering_suggestions: Filter users by start date range
Filter by userStatus to analyze active vs. inactive users
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of users by start date
Average tenure calculation based on start date

Occasional write access during employee onboarding and status updates

primary_use_cases: Managerial oversight of employee status and hierarchy

semantic_description.description: The User_csod table serves as a source repository for user information within an organizational context, capturing essential details about employees and their hierarchical relationships.

key_columns.name: userId
key_columns.description: Unique identifier for each user in the system
key_columns.business_significance: Essential for distinguishing individual users and linking to other related data within the organization

key_columns.name: userName
key_columns.description: Username used for system login and identification
key_columns.business_significance: Critical for user authentication and access control within the system

key_columns.name: email
key_columns.description: User's primary email address for communication
key_columns.business_significance: Key for internal and external communication, as well as for notifications and alerts

key_columns.name: managerId
key_columns.description: Unique identifier for the user's manager
key_columns.business_significance: Facilitates the understanding of reporting structures and organizational hierarchy

key_columns.name: userStatus
key_columns.description: Current employment status of the user (e.g., active, inactive)
key_columns.business_significance: Important for HR analytics and workforce management, indicating user availability

key_columns.name: startDate
key_columns.description: Date when the user commenced employment
key_columns.business_significance: Useful for tracking tenure and calculating service-related metrics

semantic_description.table_purpose: To maintain a comprehensive record of user profiles, including personal details, employment status, and organizational structure, facilitating HR management and reporting.
semantic_description.business_context: This table is integral to human resources management, providing a structured view of employee data that supports various HR functions such as onboarding, performance management, and organizational planning. It enables the organization to maintain an accurate and up-to-date employee directory, which is essential for effective communication and resource allocation.

suggested_relationships.reasoning: Each user belongs to a specific division, allowing for departmental reporting and resource management
Each user can have multiple activity logs, enabling tracking of user engagement and system usage

performance_considerations: Ensure efficient indexing on userId and managerId for quick lookups
Monitor query performance during peak access times to maintain responsiveness

recommendations: At least 2 tables are required for relationship analysis

tables.name: PerformanceData_csod
tables.description: This table contains detailed performance data for employees, including task assignments, ratings, and status updates within the Cornerstone Talent Performance module.

columns.name: userId
columns.description: Identifier for users in the performance data table

business_rules: Can be null
Should not be used as a primary key
data_quality_checks: Check for null values
Validate format against expected user ID patterns
metadata.business_description: Unique identifier assigned to each user for tracking performance data
filtering_suggestions: Filter by user ID to analyze specific user performance

columns.name: taskName
columns.description: The name of the task assigned to an employee within the performance management system.

business_rules: Can be null
Should accurately reflect the task being performed
data_quality_checks: Ensure task names are descriptive and relevant
Check for consistency in naming conventions
metadata.business_description: This column represents the specific task that is part of the performance evaluation process for employees.

columns.name: taskId
columns.description: Identifier for each task within the performance data.

business_rules: Can be null
Should be unique within the context of a specific performance review
data_quality_checks: Check for null values
Validate format to ensure it is a valid string representation of a bigint
metadata.business_description: Unique identifier for tasks assigned to employees in the performance management system.

columns.name: taskFinalRating
columns.description: Final rating assigned to a task based on performance evaluation.

business_rules: Can be null if the task has not been rated yet
Should reflect a valid rating scale defined by the organization
data_quality_checks: Ensure ratings are within the defined scale (e.g., 1 to 5)
Check for consistency with related performance metrics
metadata.business_description: This column represents the final rating given to a specific task, reflecting the performance assessment of the employee on that task.

columns.name: taskStatus
columns.description: Indicates the current status of a task within the performance evaluation process.

business_rules: Can be null
Should reflect valid task statuses such as 'Not Started', 'In Progress', 'Completed', 'On Hold'
data_quality_checks: Ensure values are from a predefined list of valid statuses
Check for null values where applicable
metadata.business_description: This column represents the status of a specific task assigned to an employee, which can be used to track progress and completion in performance evaluations.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by userId to see individual task statuses
aggregation_suggestions: Percentage of tasks in each status

columns.name: stepAssignedTo
columns.description: Indicates the user or employee to whom a specific step in the performance task is assigned.

business_rules: Can be null if the step is not assigned to anyone
Should reference a valid userId from the userId column
data_quality_checks: Check for valid userId references
Ensure no invalid characters are present in the text
metadata.business_description: This column represents the individual responsible for completing a particular step in the performance evaluation process.

columns.name: stepTitle
columns.description: Title of the performance step associated with a task.

business_rules: Can be null
Should accurately reflect the step's purpose
data_quality_checks: Ensure consistency in naming conventions
Check for excessive length
metadata.business_description: This column represents the title of a specific step within a performance task, providing context for the evaluation process.

columns.name: stepStatus
columns.description: Indicates the current status of a performance step within the talent performance process.

business_rules: Can be null
Should reflect valid status options such as 'Not Started', 'In Progress', 'Completed', etc.
data_quality_checks: Ensure values are within predefined status options
Check for null values where applicable
metadata.business_description: This column represents the status of a specific step in the performance evaluation process, which can help in tracking progress and identifying bottlenecks.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by userId to see individual performance steps

columns.name: startDate
columns.description: The date when the performance task starts, represented as a string in timestamp format without time zone.

business_rules: Can be null if the task has not started yet
Should follow the format 'YYYY-MM-DD' for consistency
data_quality_checks: Validate format to ensure it matches 'YYYY-MM-DD'
Check for logical consistency with other date fields (e.g., startDate should be before completionDate)
metadata.business_description: Indicates the initiation date of a performance-related task for an employee.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by tasks that started within a certain date range
aggregation_suggestions: COUNT of tasks started within a specific period
Average duration from startDate to completionDate

columns.name: completionDate
columns.description: Date when the task was completed, represented as a timestamp without time zone.

business_rules: Can be null if the task is not completed
Should be in a valid date format
data_quality_checks: Validate date format
Check for logical consistency with startDate and stepDueDate
metadata.business_description: This column indicates the date on which a specific task was completed by an employee, providing insights into task performance and timelines.

columns.name: expirationDate
columns.description: The date when a performance task or evaluation expires.

business_rules: Can be null if the task does not have an expiration date
Should be in a valid date format (YYYY-MM-DD)
data_quality_checks: Validate date format
Check for logical consistency with startDate and completionDate
metadata.business_description: This column indicates the expiration date for a specific performance task, which is relevant for tracking the validity of performance evaluations.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by tasks that are expired
Filter by tasks that are active

columns.name: stepDueDate
columns.description: The date by which a specific step in the performance task is due.

business_rules: Can be null
Should be in valid date format
data_quality_checks: Check for valid date format
Ensure dates are not in the past unless specified
metadata.business_description: This column represents the deadline for completing a specific step in the performance evaluation process.

columns.name: stepSubmissionDate
columns.description: The date when the step was submitted in the performance evaluation process.

business_rules: Can be null if the step has not been submitted yet
Should be in a valid date format
data_quality_checks: Validate date format
Check for null values where submission is expected
metadata.business_description: This column represents the date on which a specific step in the performance evaluation process was submitted by the employee.
filtering_suggestions: Filter by userId to see submissions by individual employees

columns.name: userId
columns.description: Column userId

columns.name: taskName
columns.description: Column taskName

columns.name: taskId
columns.description: Column taskId

columns.name: taskFinalRating
columns.description: Column taskFinalRating

columns.name: taskStatus
columns.description: Column taskStatus

columns.name: stepAssignedTo
columns.description: Column stepAssignedTo

columns.name: stepTitle
columns.description: Column stepTitle

columns.name: stepStatus
columns.description: Column stepStatus

columns.name: startDate
columns.description: Column startDate

columns.name: completionDate
columns.description: Column completionDate

columns.name: expirationDate
columns.description: Column expirationDate

columns.name: stepDueDate
columns.description: Column stepDueDate

columns.name: stepSubmissionDate
columns.description: Column stepSubmissionDate

Connected to task management processes for workflow tracking
primary_use_cases: Tracking employee task performance and completion rates
Evaluating employee performance ratings for reviews
Monitoring task status and deadlines for timely interventions

semantic_description.description: The PerformanceData_csod table serves as a source for tracking and managing performance-related tasks within an organizational context, capturing detailed information about user assignments, task statuses, and timelines.

key_columns.name: userId
key_columns.description: Unique identifier for each user in the system
key_columns.business_significance: Essential for linking performance data to specific users, enabling personalized performance tracking and reporting.

key_columns.name: taskName
key_columns.description: Name of the task assigned to the user
key_columns.business_significance: Helps in identifying the specific performance tasks and their relevance to user roles and objectives.

key_columns.name: taskFinalRating
key_columns.description: Final rating assigned to the task upon completion
key_columns.business_significance: Critical for evaluating user performance and providing feedback for improvement.

key_columns.name: taskStatus
key_columns.description: Current status of the task (e.g., In Progress, Completed)
key_columns.business_significance: Indicates the progress of tasks, allowing for effective monitoring and management of performance workflows.

key_columns.name: startDate
key_columns.description: Date when the task was initiated
key_columns.business_significance: Important for tracking task timelines and understanding performance duration.

key_columns.name: completionDate
key_columns.description: Date when the task was completed
key_columns.business_significance: Used to assess task efficiency and overall performance metrics.

semantic_description.table_purpose: To provide a comprehensive overview of performance tasks assigned to users, including their statuses, ratings, and key dates for effective performance management and reporting.
semantic_description.business_context: This table is utilized within performance management systems to monitor and evaluate employee tasks and performance outcomes. It supports HR and management teams in assessing productivity, identifying training needs, and facilitating performance reviews.

suggested_relationships.reasoning: Each user can have multiple performance tasks, allowing for a comprehensive view of individual performance across various assignments.
Tasks may be defined in a separate table, linking task details to their definitions for consistency and clarity in performance tracking.

performance_considerations: Ensure efficient querying for real-time performance tracking
Optimize data storage to handle large volumes of performance records

recommendations: At least 2 tables are required for relationship analysis
